macro_summary: Markets open Tuesday in mild risk-off mode: S&P futures -0.28%, Nasdaq futures -0.32%, with the 10-year yield briefly touching 4.5% overnight. The dominant macro backdrop remains a hawkish Fed pivot — Chair Warsh's June 17 meeting removed the easing bias, with 9 of 18 officials now projecting at least one rate hike in 2026 and the median dot rising to 3.8% — while a U.S.-Iran peace framework (60-day oil license, Strait of Hormuz reopening) is unwinding the energy-war premium that drove inflation to 4.2% CPI in May. The week's two sharpest catalysts are both earnings-driven: FedEx reports tonight (June 23 AMC) and Micron reports Wednesday night (June 24 AMC). Thursday's May PCE print (Wells Fargo forecasts 4.1% YoY headline, 3.4% core) is the key macro wildcard that could either cement or complicate September rate-hike pricing.
key_events: FedEx (FDX) Q4 FY2026 earnings tonight after close — first report post-Freight spinoff
Micron (MU) Q3 FY2026 earnings Wednesday June 24 after close — biggest AI memory event of the month
May PCE inflation data release Wednesday June 25 (BEA confirmed); Wells Fargo forecasts headline 4.1% YoY
Q1 GDP third estimate also due Thursday June 25
$69B 2-year Treasury auction tomorrow — result could reset short-end yields and Sept hike pricing
10-year yield hit 4.5% overnight, highest since June 12, as hawkish Fed and sticky inflation dominate
Iran 60-day oil license issued; Strait of Hormuz shipping recovering but energy market normalization expected to take months
S&P futures -0.28%, Nasdaq futures -0.32%, VIX +2.32% to 16.78 pre-market

ideas.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Micron reports Q3 FY2026 earnings Wednesday June 24 after close, with consensus at ~$19.72 EPS and $34.4-35.4B revenue — figures that would have been 'unimaginable 18 months ago' per analysts. Micron has beaten EPS consensus in each of the last four quarters by an average of 21.7%, its entire 2026 HBM capacity is fully contracted, and management guided to ~81% gross margins for Q3 — one of the highest in company history. The stock trades at roughly 10x FY2027 consensus EPS of ~$97, a dramatic discount to the semiconductor sector's ~31x average forward P/E, reflecting residual cyclical skepticism that a structural AI memory model may be eroding.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings report Wednesday June 24, 2026 after market close, followed by a call with CEO Sanjay Mehrotra on HBM4 allocation visibility into 2027 and Nvidia Vera Rubin ramp
ideas.edge: Forward P/E on FY2027 consensus EPS (~$97) is ~10x vs semiconductor sector average of ~31x. Micron has averaged a 21.7% positive EPS surprise over the last four quarters. Bernstein raised MU price target to $1,625 (UBS); average analyst target ~$1,091 with 27 of 27 analysts at Strong Buy or Buy and zero Sell ratings. HBM supply is 100% contracted through 2026 with multi-year LTAs replacing spot pricing.
ideas.bull_case: A Q3 beat + strong Q4/FY2027 HBM guidance would validate the secular AI memory re-rating thesis, potentially compressing the ~10x FY27 P/E meaningfully toward sector norms. The DRAM supply-demand gap is pegged by Goldman Sachs at 4.9% — the most severe shortage in 15 years — directly supporting Micron's pricing power through at least 2027.
ideas.bear_case: Goldman Sachs maintains a bear target near $400, arguing memory remains deeply cyclical and today's ~81% gross margins are a cycle peak. Synchronized capacity expansion from SK Hynix and Samsung could compress pricing faster than multi-year contracts suggest, and a single weak hyperscaler capex revision could crack sentiment on a stock already up 300%+ YTD.

ideas.name: FedEx Corporation
ideas.thesis: FedEx reports Q4 FY2026 results tonight after close — the first earnings as a pure-play parcel company following the June 1 Freight spinoff. Consensus expects $5.91 EPS on $24.18B revenue (+8.8% YoY), but the more accurate Zacks 'Most Accurate Estimate' sits at $6.13 (+3.76% Earnings ESP), and Bernstein expects a ~3% beat driven by Network 2.0 cost savings and stable air cargo rates. The Q4 call will set the tone for FY2027 guidance, the post-spin reporting structure, and monetization of FedEx's retained 19.9% FDXF stake.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings report tonight June 23, 2026 after market close; FY2027 guidance and first post-Freight-spinoff financial framework will be the market-moving elements
ideas.edge: Bernstein lowered price target to $424 but kept Outperform — still ~30% upside from ~$326 current price. Zacks Earnings ESP of +3.76% (Most Accurate Estimate $6.13 vs consensus $5.91) with a Strong Buy rating flags a statistically elevated beat probability. FDX trades at 17.4x P/E with a Zacks Value Score of B and a forward P/S at a discount to both the industry average and UPS. Full-year FY2026 EPS consensus revised up 0.9% to $19.78 in the last 60 days, an 8.7% improvement over FY2025.
ideas.bull_case: A beat on EPS + FY2027 guidance above $21+ EPS would confirm that Network 2.0 is delivering real structural margin lift and that the slimmer, Freight-free FDX is re-ratable toward a premium multiple. The Amazon oversized-parcel contract (gained as UPS reduced Amazon volumes) is a multi-year volume tailwind not yet fully priced in.
ideas.bear_case: FDX has already rallied ~40-90% from its 52-week low, so the bar for a positive post-earnings reaction is elevated. $18.6M in insider selling over the past three months is a yellow flag, and global trade headwinds from inflation and tariff uncertainty could disappoint on volume guidance. Short-term technicals are bearish with stock below its 10-day and 20-day moving averages heading into the print.

ideas.name: United Airlines Holdings, Inc.
ideas.thesis: The U.S.-Iran peace framework and resulting 60-day Iranian oil license have sent jet fuel spot prices to $2.85/gallon, down sharply from an April high of $4.88 — a structural cost relief catalyst for airlines that had seen fuel bills surge 121% YoY by April 2026. Critically, tight U.S. domestic capacity (seat growth only +0.4% YoY in Q3) means airlines can likely pocket fuel savings as margin improvement rather than ceding it in a fare war, a dynamic specifically flagged by J.P. Morgan. Jefferies estimates each 5% drop in jet fuel prices lifts United EPS by 10-15%.
ideas.catalyst: Ongoing Strait of Hormuz reopening and normalization of Iranian oil exports over the next 60-day license window; Q2 earnings (late July) will be the first full quarter to show fuel cost relief in reported numbers
ideas.edge: Jefferies: each 5% decline in $3/gal fuel forecast adds 10-15% to UAL EPS. Domestic capacity constrained at +0.4% YoY limits fare-war risk per J.P. Morgan. Average domestic fares were up 34.1% YoY as of June 8 (Raymond James data), meaning fare revenue is sticky even as costs fall — a rare double-positive setup. IATA's $98B fuel-bill overhang for 2026 had been the sector's biggest drag; it is now mechanically shrinking.
ideas.bull_case: If oil stabilizes at or below $77/barrel and fare discipline holds through summer peak season, United's Q2 and Q3 margins could deliver a substantial positive surprise vs. pre-war consensus estimates. CEO Scott Kirby stated the airline is 'on a path to recovering 100% of fuel costs through pricing by year-end,' meaning fuel savings accrue almost entirely to margin.
ideas.bear_case: Jet fuel still costs 54% more than a year ago even after the decline, and airline fuel hedges mean spot-price relief takes quarters to fully appear in reported COGS. Iran deal remains fragile — any renewed hostility could reverse oil gains instantly. The initial UAL stock reaction (+4.5% on June 15-16) may have already front-run a significant portion of the near-term upside.
